summary: Fix cargo test's Windows compile break — consolidate the triplicated SDK-vendoring test helper into tests/common, using a junction (not a Unix symlink) for the npm-install stand-in

new.description: New shared test-support module hoisting the byte-identical `sdk_root()`/`ensure_sdk_built()` today triplicated verbatim (tests/emit.rs:628-641, tests/install.rs:57-70, tests/builtin_lock_frozen.rs:37-50), plus a new `vendor_sdk(node_modules_scope: &Path)`: ensures the `@dtmd` scope dir exists, is idempotent (skip if the link/junction already exists — install.rs's existing guard, generalized), calls `ensure_sdk_built()`, then links `sdk_root()` into it as the stand-in for a real `npm install`'s local-dependency resolution. `#[cfg(unix)]` keeps `std::os::unix::fs::symlink(target, link)`; `#[cfg(windows)]` shells `cmd /C mklink /J <link> <target>` — note mklink's arg order is Link-then-Target, reversed vs symlink's (original, link), and quote paths since CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR may contain spaces. A junction (never `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir`) needs no SeCreateSymbolicLinkPrivilege/Developer Mode, matching how npm itself links local/workspace deps on Windows — cite npm/cli#5189, nixhacker.com 'Understanding and Exploiting Symbolic links in Windows', hinchley.net 'Junctions and Symbolic Links', all retrieved 2026-07-08, into the code comment (rust.md comment bar).

edit.description: Add `mod common;`; delete the local `sdk_root`/`ensure_sdk_built` (lines 628-641); in `wire_sdk_harness_program` (line 699) replace the unconditional `std::os::unix::fs::symlink(sdk_root(), node_modules_scope.join("temper"))` with `common::vendor_sdk(&node_modules_scope)`.

Add `mod common;`; delete the local `sdk_root`/`ensure_sdk_built` (lines 57-70) and the local `vendor_sdk` (lines 76-86, unconditional symlink at line 84) — its one caller uses `common::vendor_sdk` instead.

Add `mod common;`; delete the local `sdk_root`/`ensure_sdk_built` (lines 37-50); in `wire_memberless_harness` (line 105) replace the unconditional `std::os::unix::fs::symlink(...)` with `common::vendor_sdk(&node_modules_scope)`.

tests.asserts: the real-SDK harness tests (e.g. `wire_sdk_harness`) still pass unchanged on unix after the extraction — same symlink behavior via the shared helper
install's real-SDK-vendored tests still pass unchanged after `vendor_sdk` moves to tests/common
the frozen-lock byte-compare test against the memberless harness still passes after `wire_memberless_harness` uses the shared helper

acceptance: `cargo test` compiles and the full suite runs to completion on Windows — no unconditional unix-only symlink call remains in tests/; on unix, `vendor_sdk`'s behavior and the full suite (229/229 per the field report) are unchanged.
notes: Windows field report observed at baac085; junction-not-symlink_dir fix verified 2026-07-08 (nixhacker.com, hinchley.net, npm/cli#5189 — carry cites into the code comment, rust.md). No new crate: shell `cmd /C mklink /J <link> <target>` (arg order reversed vs std::os::unix::fs::symlink's (original, link)). CI is ubuntu-only — build can't execute the Windows path itself; say so in the commit body. scoped at 16f844b

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper`, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`, now at 0.0.5 pending publish — SDK-VERSION-LOCKSTEP); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped. No `.github/workflows/release.yml` exists (only the `temper.yml` check job); root package.json is still the private flume manifest.

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (distribution.md).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, distribution.md). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (distribution.md 'Fail-loud invariant').
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm `@dtmd/temper`; ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. install.rs range-correctness shipped in SCAFFOLD-OUTPUT-VALID (^0.0.4); release owns only future re-pins.
